explanation: which have their own category. See also new FIRE-TURBULENT category.

provenance.reason: the reasoned gate rejected 33% of this subcategory's own true clips

discriminators: The roar, not the crackle. The explanation is explicit: 'mostly roars', 'not isolate fire crackle'. That maps onto one axis — BURNING is broadband and STATIONARY (high stationarity, low spectral_flux, low crest_factor); CRACKLE is the same burning material but eventful (high flux, spiky crest, sparse resolvable transients). Score that axis, not the timbre.
Lower spectral_centroid_hz, far lower onset_rate_per_second and much more low_band_energy than SIZZLE, which is a dense high-frequency fizz.

Less low-frequency weight, lower spectral_flux and much lower level than TURBULENT, which is the same physics driven violently. The explanation's own cross-reference to FIRE-TURBULENT is exactly this boundary.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: A sharp and loud release of fire
big flare ups. Think a backdraft explosion of fire. A flamethrower. Usually a single event.

discriminators: 'A sharp and loud release of fire... Usually a single event.' A backdraft: one large front-loaded broadband transient. Fast envelope_attack_seconds, high crest_factor, low envelope_temporal_centroid, near-zero sustain_ratio.
Against WHOOSH — the load-bearing sibling boundary: WHOOSH swells (envelope_attack_seconds one to two orders slower, roughly half the crest_factor, a swept centroid trajectory). BURST detonates.

Against BURNING: BURNING has no dominant transient and is stationary. Against IGNITE: same shape, but IGNITE is orders of magnitude quieter, shorter and brighter — a match strike, not a fireball, so it lacks the low-frequency combustion body.
Against EXPLOSIONS-REAL: identical morphology, and DSP does not separate them. The fire timbre (a sustained combustion roar in the tail rather than a debris field) plus name evidence is all there is.

explanation: good for layering to other fire.

discriminators: A SPARSE, IRREGULAR impulse train. Two features define it: a moderate onset_rate_per_second (single digits per second, not tens) and a LOW onset_periodicity — fire pops are stochastic. A high onset_periodicity at this onset rate is a clock, a machine or an engine idle, never a fire. That is the single most diagnostic pairing in the category.
The transients are high-frequency-weighted (dry fibre snapping) and the file is quiet between them: high crest_factor, high envelope_kurtosis, low sustain_ratio, low absolute level.

Against BURNING — the flux/stationarity axis, read the other way: CRACKLE = high spectral_flux, low stationarity, resolvable events. BURNING = low flux, high stationarity, no events. The explanation writes this boundary itself ('not isolate fire crackle... which have their own category').

Against SIZZLE: SIZZLE's onset_rate_per_second is an order of magnitude higher (tens to hundreds per second), dense enough to fuse into a continuous hiss, with far more high_band_energy. CRACKLE's pops are individually countable.

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: Gas burners as you'd find in a kitchen stove or gas grill. That sort of hissy or airy fire burn. Crematoriums and furnaces.

discriminators: 'That sort of hissy or airy fire burn.' Physically a premixed-flame jet: stationary broadband noise with the energy shifted upward — high high_band_energy, high spectral_centroid_hz, high spectral_flatness, near-zero spectral_flux. It is BURNING with the crackle and most of the low-frequency roar removed.

THE BOUNDARY WITH AIR-HISS IS THE HARD ONE, AND DSP LOSES IT. A propane burner and a compressed-air leak are both stationary high-frequency noise; on flatness, centroid, crest and stationarity they are the same object. The only signal cue is a residual low-frequency combustion rumble under the hiss (low_band_energy meaningfully above zero, where a pure pneumatic hiss has almost none), and it is weak. With no low-frequency component and no name evidence (Propane, Burner, Stove, Blowtorch, Furnace, Bunsen), prefer AIRHiss. Never assert FIREGas on hiss alone.
The same boundary and the same verdict apply against WATER-STEAM and GEOTHERMAL-FUMAROLE — all four are one stationary hiss wearing different labels.
Against SIZZLE: SIZZLE retains resolvable micro-transients (higher crest_factor, higher spectral_flux). A gas jet is smooth.

explanation: lighters. That initial spark of ignition of a fire.

discriminators: 'That initial spark of ignition of a fire.' A match or a lighter is a short, quiet, high-frequency-weighted front-loaded event: a friction scrape or a flint click, then a small gas whoof. Low envelope_temporal_centroid, high crest_factor, high high_band_energy, very low absolute level.
Against BURST: the same morphology, but BURST is a fireball — orders of magnitude louder and longer, with real low_band_energy. Level and low-frequency weight are the separator.

Honest limit: a match strike is acoustically a small bright friction transient and is not reliably separable from a generic small strike, an electrical spark or a wood snap. The signature narrows the field to short bright transients; the synonyms (Match, Lighter, Zippo, Ignite, Spark) must commit it.

explanation: Fire sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: Catch-all / abstention target. It has no morphology of its own: it is the answer when no sibling in this category wins decisively (spec section 6). No gates and no priors — asserting MISC from positive signal evidence would be incoherent.

'Fire sounds not fitting another category in this list.' Reached through the FIRE category axis (timbre or text) followed by a failure to commit to any FIRE sibling — not by positive evidence of its own.

explanation: sauna rocks when water is poured on them. Glowing red hot.

discriminators: DENSE high-frequency crackle. The one number that matters: onset_rate_per_second is an order of magnitude above CRACKLE — tens to hundreds of micro-events per second, dense enough that they fuse into a bright continuous fizz (stationarity climbs, crest_factor falls). CRACKLE's pops are countable; SIZZLE's are not.

Spectrally the brightest thing in FIRE: high_band_energy dominant, low_band_energy near zero, spectral_centroid_hz up in the kilohertz. Water flashing off a hot surface, a burning fuse, a quenching ember.

Against GAS and AIR-HISS: those are smooth jets with no micro-transient structure. SIZZLE keeps a measurably higher crest_factor and spectral_flux than a pure hiss — that residual spikiness is the only thing separating a dense texture from a noise bed.
Against BURNING: BURNING is low-centroid and low-flux. There is no overlap in the high/low band ratio.

explanation: that phasey fire sound as a torch is moved around.

discriminators: 'That phasey fire sound as a torch is moved around.' A small BURNING with a strong flame-flap modulation: mid-range spectral_flux and a high spectral_centroid_deviation_hz — the spectrum wobbles as the flame is dragged through the air — where BURNING's centroid sits still.

It lives between BURNING (static, larger, more low-frequency) and TURBULENT (violent, far louder). Scale is the only separator from BURNING and it is weak: a torch is a small fire. Requires name evidence (Torch, Brazier, Flambeau, Sconce, Wick) to commit.
The movement is modulation, not events: onset_rate_per_second stays low, unlike CRACKLE, whose energy arrives as discrete impulses.

explanation: Wildly whooshing turbulent violent fire. Larger than just burning
think fire tornado. Extreme moving fire.

discriminators: 'Wildly whooshing turbulent violent fire. Larger than just burning.' Loud, low-frequency-heavy, high-flux, NON-stationary roar. Against BURNING this is the same axis read the other way: BURNING sits at high stationarity and low flux; TURBULENT sits at low stationarity and high flux, with far more low_band_energy and level.

Honest limit: a fire tornado and a wind tornado are the same broadband low-frequency churn. WIND-TURBULENT, the NATURAL DISASTER composites and this are not separable by DSP — the combustion crackle layer is the only cue and it is usually buried. Text decides.
Against BURST: BURST is a single front-loaded event. TURBULENT is a sustained scene of many swells — no dominant transient, envelope_temporal_centroid near the middle.

explanation: Big whooshy fire events like a fireball through the air.

discriminators: 'Big whooshy fire events like a fireball through the air.' A SWEPT broadband noise: the centroid traces a monotonic trajectory (spectral_centroid_slope_hz_per_second — up on approach, down on departure) and the envelope SWELLS rather than detonating.
Against BURST — this is the load-bearing boundary: envelope_attack_seconds is one to two orders of magnitude slower and crest_factor roughly half. A burst is a transient; a whoosh is a trajectory.

Against SWOOSHES-WHOOSH and DESIGNED-WHOOSH: identical morphology, and DSP cannot separate them on shape. Only the fire timbre — a broadband combustion roar with real low_band_energy, not a filtered noise sweep — plus name evidence (Fireball, Molotov, Backdraft) distinguishes them.
